name: Homemade Teriyaki Marinade
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Sake (or dry white wine or vodka)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine 1 cup of brown sugar, 1 cup of soy sauce, and 1 cup of sake in a bowl.
2. Grate the fresh ginger root and add it to the mixture.
3. Add minced garlic to the marinade.
4. Place thin cut strips of steak or chicken parts (especially large quantities of wings) into the marinade and soak overnight.
5. For best results, grill on the BBQ, but baking is also an option at 350°F for about 1 hour per pound.
6. For a simpler alternative, drizzle Italian dressing over a cheap steak to add variety in flavor. No specific tenderizing qualities are guaranteed with this method.
Note: The most effective marinade I've found for tenderizing meat has been a homemade Teriyaki recipe from a college roommate in Hawaii. The high alcohol content seems to play a key role.
